findings.message: ItemRead exposes owner_email (PII) and is the response_model for both GET /items and POST /items, so the owner's email is echoed in external API responses. This directly contradicts the data-handling annotation added to the model in this same diff: 'PII: owner email address — do not include in logs or external responses.'
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from ItemRead (the externally-serialized schema). If owners must see their own contact, gate it behind an authorized/owner-only response model rather than returning it on the general list/create endpoints.

findings.message: ItemCreate collects owner_email (PII) from the request body, but post_item calls create_item(session, payload.name) and never passes/persists owner_email. PII is accepted over the wire (and may be captured by request logging) with no use for the stated purpose — unnecessary collection.
findings.suggestion: Either drop owner_email from ItemCreate if it is not needed, or, if it is genuinely required, pass it through to create_item so collection is justified by actual use; ensure request-body logging does not capture this field.
